reviews.review_description: A must-have 3d vr game

reviews.review_title: One of the best games on GO
reviews.review_description: This game is a technical masterpiece and as a actual game pretty good. Its a challenging Indie jump and run with cool hi-res graphics and great sound design. You need a (Xbox one) Controller to play it.

Hav a psvr with almost all games. This still manages to stunn me in therms of sharpnes and scale. Beside that it is a REAL game, hard to find with all the algorithm driven suggestions. LOVE it!!
Hope to see more from you developers, probably a metroid style game with the same kind of perspective? Anyway thanks for that gem, keep on that good work! Gonna promote this game as mich as possible. Bless!
Can't get it to stat, says it can't detect a controller. Using a Oculus Go.

developer_response.body: Assuming you are using a gamepad, did you manage to get the game working? If not, you can always reach out to us through support@houseofsecrets.nl to see if we can resolve the issue.

reviews.review_description: This game is a true gem not only in gameplay. This is one of very few games that allows for graphics settings to be changed. On the Oculus Go this game looks amazing and is just so fun to play. Feels like I'm controlling someone in a micro machines world, has the miniature look and feel. Very addicting. Seemed very hard to play at first because it wasn't clear how to fight and what to do. Didn't tell too long however to figure it out and now it's one of my favorite games. Highly recommended.

Kinda reminds me of Hyper Light Drifter estethically and combat-wise. Great boss fights, nice plot told without words. Completed in few hours, but there is a bunch of different worlds to complete, so it’s fine. Hope to see more from this developer!

Like Brusanan says below.. if you're on the Oculus Go, change the graphics settings. You'll be pleasantly surprised.

Atmosphere is intoxicating. Character reminds me of Allumette in the way she looks &  moves.

This game & Witchblood & Herobound games prove diorama VR is it's own genre.

KIN is a great and expansive virtual reality take on classic adventure reminiscient of Legend of Zelda and a cut above the Herobound series.  The boss battles are challenging, the designed for VR "wide" levels are immersive and the storyline without any dialogue is engaging.  I can't recommend this one enough if you have a gamepad for your Geat VR.

Runs smooth, nice artistic style, controls are tight. It is kinda hard though... but it's a "fair" hard. Like when I die I know it's not because of any issue with the game's camera or input responsivity, it's a reactionary skill I can get better at to overcome a enemy bundle or a boss.

Can be beaten in a few hours, but it felt fairly substantial.

I am enjoying it so far. 

I love the graphical options- thanks devs. 

The controls feel a bit twitchy at first, and even entering shallow water is unforgiving, but I'm getting used to that. 

Really great production values, and fun gameplay.

I have yet to reach the first boss,but I had to take a break to let everyone know how outstanding this game is. 
The love and focus the developers poured into this EXPERIENCE immediately envelops your senses. Hands down a triple AAA VR title. I will update this review as I make progress. Don't think about...just buy it now!

This is an awesome game with the best graphics ever on gear VR but the difficulty is too hard... but thank you for the trick to defeat the 1st boss but even with your advices I spent 2 hours to kill him :p Now I can't defeat the second boss... i spent all the day on it... I give up... too bad I used to love that game...

developer_response.body:
Hi Ken, we're working on bringing difficulty settings in a future update.
However, that won't help you at the moment, but I'll give a tip:



WARNING, HERE BE SPOILERS!



Whenever you hit the boss (after anticipating his jump to the other end of the arena and dodging his attack), he will try to jump on you, at this point, just walk away from him, this way he will never hit you.

reviews.review_description: I love the unique perspective of the game. Also, the controls and visual style are superb! You can really tell a lot of passion and hard work went into making this. I highly recommend this beautiful and challenging game!

This is a masterpiece! This game has all the hallmarks of a truly great game. It is challenging, artistic, and has a subtle and atmospheric soundtrack. It seems almost too difficult at first, but than you realize that this is a puzzle game where you have to think outside the norm to succeed. It is a unique game that is well suited to VR. A sincere thank you and  congratulations to the devs.

Do you like falling off ledges repeatedly, like 200 times? Do you hate your life? If so, Kin is your type of game. 

Kin = Dark Souls "You are dead" x Super Meat Boy. Restart, restart, restart. Your main character loses health even in shallow water, guess he's related to the Wicked Witch of the West. 

Game never bothers to inform you about charged melee or ranged. 

First monster rushes you and explodes with a huge AOE, second monster charges and bumps you off the stage. You always have to dodge first.
Good luck navigating the VR terrain with your compulsory controller. 
Game looks nice but don't bother buying this at full price or even half price. You can finish this in 1 go, use your money for a cup of Joe instead.

Kin is a beautiful, fun and challenging action platformer!

The world feels so alive. And man is it beautiful to look at, and interesting to explore.

The controls are tight, the UI is slick, and the options menu is the best I have seen on the Go so far. Every game should have the graphics options Kin has. One thing to keep in mind if you are playing on a Go: your hardware can handle much more than the default settings. Be sure to set anti-aliasing to 4x, and bump that resolution up to 80-90%. It makes a huge difference.

Everything about this game is well done and polished. I'd call this the Go's killer app!

reviews.review_title: My new favourite controller based game
reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing in so many ways, beautiful art, great use of 3d and perspective, almost the perfect game. The only thing I would change about it is the difficulty (though I might have read somewhere that difficulty settings are on the way, which would be great), it's fun, but it often makes me swear and yell while I play; a bit more frustrating than I can comfortably take. I'm currently stuck on the last boss and I think I'll wait until the difficulty is updated before I try fighting him again for the millionth time. A level selector, multiple save slots, or both would be great too, I'd like to show this to my friends without overriding my save or dumping them in the middle of a really tricky level.

Edit: Yay! It's been updated with difficulty settings and a level selector. I can now show this to my friends to convince them mobile VR is awesome. I've updated my rating to 5 stars, the only gripe I have now is that the later levels aren't quite as fun as the early levels, they also don't visually pop the way the first three quarters of the game do.
Really well designed, immersive, puzzle explorer, with many well designed levels.

reviews.review_title: Best game so far hands down.
reviews.review_description: Game is just magnificent. It's bastion in voxels.
I absolutely fell in love with this game. I would love to see more titles like this on the go.
